isolation_opt_out_reason: Bundled multi-feature post-merge closure in chore/v7-9-1-close-stuck-features — all 4 features were v7.9.1 build-window ships (PRs #625-#628) that landed yesterday and stuck at implementation phase due to FEATURE_CLOSURE_COMPLETENESS pr_list_parity_mismatch firing pre-PR-list-citation; chore bundling reduces PR overhead vs 4 separate post-merge-closure branches per close-feature.py default pattern
primary_metric: 5 of the remaining 7 Tier-2/Tier-3 dev-env R-items ship in a single batch as warn-only CI workflows + configs (R11 gitleaks + R13 pip-audit + R14 SBOM + R17 commitlint + R18 shellcheck)
success_metrics: R11 gitleaks: .gitleaks.toml + .github/workflows/gitleaks.yml present; warn-only (continue-on-error: true); PR + push + Sunday cron triggers (T1, presence + yaml lint)
R13 pip-audit: .github/workflows/pip-audit.yml present; paths-filtered to ai-engine/; warn-only; columns + JSON output; 14-day artifact (T1)
R14 SBOM: .github/workflows/sbom.yml present; fires on v* tag pushes; both SPDX-JSON and CycloneDX-JSON via anchore/sbom-action@v0 (T1; dormant until first release tag)
R17 commitlint: commitlint.config.js + .github/workflows/commitlint.yml present; @commitlint/config-conventional + relaxed header (150) + body line length (200); warn-only (T1)
R18 shellcheck: .github/workflows/shellcheck.yml present; via ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master against scripts/ + .githooks/pre-commit; warn-only (T1)
dev-env-master-plan-2026-05-24.md R-table updated for all 5 items (T1, measured by section presence)
kill_criteria: Warn-only baseline gates PRs accidentally — mitigated by continue-on-error: true on every workflow job; verified by yaml lint
Workflow path filters miss legitimate changes — mitigated by including the workflow file itself in every paths: filter so workflow edits trigger their own run

False-positive findings flood the operator's attention budget — mitigated by .gitleaks.toml allowlist for docs/ + case-studies/ + observed-patterns.md + test fixtures; commitlint type-enum scoped to project's actual commit types
kill_criteria_resolution: All 3 mitigated by design. (1) Every new job uses `continue-on-error: true`; lint validated all 5 YAMLs parse. (2) `paths:` filters include `.github/workflows/<name>.yml` for the relevant workflow so a workflow edit auto-tests itself on PR. (3) gitleaks allowlist covers docs/case-studies/observed-patterns/test-fixtures explicitly; commitlint type-enum lists only the 11 conventional types the project actually uses. False-positive surface is bounded.
dispatch_pattern: agent-driven (5 new files + 1 config + master-plan update)
scope_summary: Tier-2/Tier-3 dev-env hygiene batch shipping 5 warn-only baselines in one PR. (1) R11 gitleaks GH Action + .gitleaks.toml (pre-commit hook integration deferred). (2) R13 pip-audit ai-engine workflow (PR-paths-filtered + Monday weekly cron). (3) R14 syft SBOM on v* tag pushes (SPDX-JSON + CycloneDX-JSON; dormant until first release tag). (4) R17 commitlint config + PR workflow (lints every commit in PR range). (5) R18 shellcheck GH Action against scripts/ + .githooks/ (pre-commit hook integration deferred for same reason as R11). All 5 ship continue-on-error: true so a finding produces an annotation but does not gate. Dev-env-master-plan R-table updated accordingly.
